messages.content: Generate a B4 English Language Term 1 Week 1 lesson note. Style: Standard.

{
  "weekEnding": "05/09/2025",
  "strand": "Reading",
  "subStrand": "Phonics",
  "contentStandard": "B4.2.2.1",
  "indicator": "B4.2.2.1.1",
  "performanceIndicator": "Learners match sounds to their corresponding letter patterns and demonstrate understanding through oral and written activities",
  "teachingResources": "Word cards, sentence cards, letter cards, flashcards, a class library",
  "coreCompetencies": "Reading and Writing Skills; Personal Development and Leadership; Communication and Collaboration",
  "values": "Cooperation, Diligence",
  "reference": "English Language Curriculum B4 Pg. 17,19; Mastermind English B4",
  "days": [
    {
      "day": "Monday",
      "phase1": "Have learners recite the English alphabets. Show a video on phonics as they imitate the video. Example Dave & Ava.",
      "phase2": "A. READING (Phonics)\nDisplay word cards on the teacher's table. Make letter sounds to learners as you pick them in turns. Have learners repeat each sound clearly.\n\nAssessment: Play the game of 'Back to the Board' with learners. A learner sits in front of the class with his/her back to the board. Teacher writes a letter on the board for the other learners to make its sound. The learner then picks the matching word card. Divide the class into two groups to make it competitive.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? Have each learner make sounds of any letter they know. Learners match sounds to appropriate letter patterns using the word cards.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Tuesday",
      "phase1": "Have learners mention their full names and where they come from. Ask students to name important places in their community and things inside and outside the classroom.",
      "phase2": "B. GRAMMAR (Nouns)\nHave learners read simple sentences on sentence cards containing names of people, animals, events and objects. Put learners in groups to identify the nouns.\nHave learners form their own sentences using names of people, places and objects and underline the nouns.\nNote that common nouns start with small letters.\n\nAssessment: Write five sentences on the board. Ask learners to identify and underline all the nouns in each sentence individually.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? How to identify and use nouns to name people, animals, places and objects.\nAsk students to summarize the main points of the lesson.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Wednesday",
      "phase1": "Engage learners in the 'What word am I writing?' game. Put learners into pairs. The teacher writes a word in the air. Learners make the letter sound and tell the teacher the word that has been written.",
      "phase2": "C. WRITING (Penmanship)\nHave learners write letters in flick: a, t, r, d, f, s. Learners join pairs of letters: at, or, on. Have learners join letters to form words: kettle, can, catch. Learners copy sentences and short passages from the board neatly.\n\nAssessment: Ask learners to copy a short sentence from the board in their best handwriting. Peer-assess for neatness and legibility.",
      "phase3": "Ask students to summarize the main points of the lesson. Discuss what makes good handwriting.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Thursday",
      "phase1": "Learners stand in a big circle. Each learner thinks of an adjective to describe themselves starting with the first letter of their name. Example: Precious Princess, Tiny Timothy.",
      "phase2": "D. WRITING CONVENTIONS & GRAMMAR USAGE (Using Punctuation)\nProvide learners with sample texts. Ask them to identify commas in the texts.\nGuide learners on the use of the comma: before/after 'Yes' and 'No'; after addressing a person; in lists.\nExamples: Yes, please. No, thank you. Kofi, can you help me?\nHave learners copy and punctuate unpunctuated sentences.\n\nAssessment: Give learners five unpunctuated sentences to punctuate correctly in their exercise books.",
      "phase3": "Ask learners to talk about what they have learnt. Call out a few words for learners to spell from memory.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Friday",
      "phase1": "Play a vocabulary game: Teacher calls a letter and learners race to name as many words beginning with that letter as possible.",
      "phase2": "E. ORAL LANGUAGE (Songs and Rhymes)\nTeach learners a new English rhyme. Have learners recite it in groups then individually.\nLead learners to identify rhyming words in the poem.\nLearners discuss the values and messages in the rhyme.\n\nAssessment: Each learner recites at least two lines of the rhyme from memory.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? Have learners name three rhyming words they remember from the lesson.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    }
  ]
}
Generate a B4 Mathematics Term 1 Week 1 lesson note. Style: Activity-based.

{
  "weekEnding": "05/09/2025",
  "strand": "Number",
  "subStrand": "Counting, Representation & Cardinality",
  "contentStandard": "B4.1.1.1",
  "indicator": "B4.1.1.1.1",
  "performanceIndicator": "Learners model number quantities and place values for multi-digit numbers using graph sheets or multi-base materials up to 100,000",
  "teachingResources": "Counters, bundle and loose straws, base ten blocks, graph sheets, patterns made from manila cards, bundle of sticks, Ghana Cedi currency notes",
  "coreCompetencies": "Critical Thinking and Problem Solving; Communication and Collaboration; Personal Development and Leadership",
  "values": "Attention to Precision, Cooperation",
  "reference": "Mathematics Curriculum B4 Pg. 23; Mastermind Maths B4",
  "days": [
    {
      "day": "Tuesday",
      "phase1": "Play 'Show Me a Number' game with learners using fingers. Teacher mentions a number from 1 to 10. Learners show the correct number of fingers.",
      "phase2": "Have learners model number quantities up to 100,000 using graph sheets or multi-base materials.\nWith multi-base blocks: cube = 100 units, rod = 1,000, flat = 10,000, block = 100,000.\nDemonstrate: model 32,300 with appropriate materials.\nLearners model given numbers using their materials in pairs.\n\nAssessment: Write three numbers on the board (e.g. 45,200; 13,050; 72,400). Learners model each number using graph sheets or counters.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? Modelling number quantities using graph sheets and multi-base materials.\nLearners model a number written on the board independently.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Wednesday",
      "phase1": "Paste a chart of base blocks on the board. Distribute papers to learners to draw what they see. Review the previous lesson through examples.",
      "phase2": "Distribute graph sheets. Demonstrate how to model the number 12,500 by shading.\nNote: cube = 100 units, rod = 1,000, flat = 10,000.\nHave learners shade graph sheets to model various numbers.\nDistribute teacher-made token currency notes in different colours. Learners model amounts up to GHC 10,000.\n\nAssessment: Give each group a number. Groups shade graph sheets and present their work to the class.",
      "phase3": "Review lesson with learners. Have a learner model a number quantity for multi-digit using multi-base materials.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Thursday",
      "phase1": "Bring real Ghana Cedi currency of different denominations. Place them in a box. Call out an amount. Learners race to model it. Do it in groups to create competition.",
      "phase2": "Read out number figures and have learners write numbers in figures and in words up to 100,000.\nExamples: 23,450 = Twenty-three thousand, four hundred and fifty.\nPlay the place value number wheel game: Throw pebbles onto the number wheel, identify place values of each digit.\n\nAssessment: Read five numbers. Learners write them in words in their exercise books.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? Writing numbers in figures and in words up to 100,000.\nEngage learners in a think-pair-share activity: write a number in figures and in words.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    },
    {
      "day": "Friday",
      "phase1": "Play 'Clap That Number'. Teacher mentions a number from 1 to 10. Learners count in unison and clap the correct number of times.",
      "phase2": "Ask learners to write numbers in expanded form.\nExample: 14,031 = 10,000 + 4,000 + 30 + 1.\nHave learners practise more examples in groups.\nLearners create their own 5-digit numbers and write them in expanded form.\n\nAssessment: Learners solve five expanded form exercises individually: e.g. 23,450 = ___ + ___ + ___ + ___.",
      "phase3": "What have we learnt today? Writing numbers in expanded form.\nCall individual learners to write a number on the board in expanded form.\nGive learners task to complete while you go round the class to support those who might need extra help."
    }
  ]
}
Generate a B7 English Language Term 1 Week 1 JHS lesson note. Style: Standard.

{
  "weekEnding": "12/01/2024",
  "day": "Thursday",
  "duration": "60mins",
  "classSize": "35",
  "strand": "Oral Language",
  "subStrand": "Listening and Speaking",
  "contentStandard": "B7.1.1.1",
  "indicator": "B7.1.1.1.1",
  "lessonNumber": "1 of 1",
  "performanceIndicator": "Learners listen actively and respond accurately to spoken texts on diverse topics and issues",
  "coreCompetencies": "Communication and Collaboration; Personal Development and Leadership; Critical Thinking and Problem Solving",
  "reference": "English Language CCP B7-B9 Pg. 2; Mastermind English B7",
  "keywords": "active listening, verbal response, register, context",
  "phase1": "Revise with learners on the previous lessons. Share performance indicators with learners and introduce the lesson.\nAsk learners: 'What happens when you listen carefully to someone speaking? What do good listeners do?'\nLearners share ideas in pairs before presenting to the class.",
  "phase2": "Identify the key features of active listening: eye contact, nodding, not interrupting, asking clarifying questions.\nModel poor listening vs active listening with a volunteer learner.\nPlay a short recorded speech or read a passage aloud. Learners listen and take notes of key points.\nIn groups, learners discuss: What was the main message? What details do you remember? What questions would you ask the speaker?\nGroups present their responses to the class.\nLearners practise giving verbal responses in formal and informal contexts.\n\nAssessment\nPairs role-play a listening and responding scenario. One learner speaks for 2 minutes on a topic (e.g. environmental pollution in Accra). The other listens actively and responds with 3 key points and 2 questions.",
  "phase3": "Use peer discussion and effective questioning to find out from learners what they have learnt during the lesson.\nTake feedback from learners and summarise the lesson.\nAsk learners: 'How will active listening help you in your studies and daily life in Ghana?'",
  "resources": "Word cards, sentence cards, letter cards, handwriting on a manila card; recorded audio clips"
}
